sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 13.21 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.13)

statement.latex: b) What is the minimal possible rate of growth of the function$(n)= _{ (g) n} |g|$for the class of groups indicated in part ``a'' of this problem (see Archive)? It is known (R.\,I.\,Grigorchuk, Math. USSR--Izv., 25 (1985), 259--300) that there exist$p 0.2ex$-groups with$(n) n^{ }$for some$>0$. At the same time, it follows from a result of E.\,I.\,Zel'manov that$(n)$is not bounded if$G$is infinite.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
